$comment: Mapping from the third-party BulletPack folder onto this engine's NATIVE strip surfaces, consumed by tools/import-bulletpack.ts. Every path is relative to the BulletPack root (argv[2], default ~/Downloads/BulletPack). This round targets the self-describing native format (design-native-format-amendment §Importer): the importer emits assets.bullets = { sheet, strips } (one packed PNG, every strip placed with x/y/frameW/frameH/frames/stride/color) plus assets.effects (one PNG per explosion strip). There is NO ship entry — player/enemy/boss 形象 are out of scope by user directive (2026-07-22); the ship seam stays engine machinery. Metadata is EXPLICIT per strip (never a filename-suffix parser — 47% of BulletPack files carry no parseable _stripN and several wide files are choice-sheets of unrelated designs, e.g. Medium_Pink_Purple and P1_Hyper_Bullet, resolved here by an explicit crop / frame count).
$grammar: The base four-stage campaign is the UNQUALIFIED consumer of the pack's baked colour (src/render/procedural.ts BULLET_VARIANTS): it fires 33 tint-free variant names of the form <baseCell>.<family> (e.g. orb.medium.decree, needle.column, glow.small.bolt), and nativeBulletAtlas keeps a pack strip named by one of those names over the floor-cell alias. So a baked design REACHES the four-stage game the moment its strip is keyed by the name that family fires — no companion content pack needed. THREE tiers (amendment §Naming, as evolved by BULLET_VARIANTS): (1) FLOOR — the 16 built-in BULLET_CELLS: whitened to luminance (rgb→255, alpha kept) so the base campaign's per-instance hue tint still colour-codes the shared cells, declared color:'tinted', animation frames PRESERVED, each fit to a per-cell coherent size (NOT the retired uniform 28px regrid). (2) VARIANTS — enemy/boss fired names / (3) SHOTS — player weapon+option fired names: the coloured native art declared color:'baked' at PIXEL-EXACT native size (no whiten, no resample; only lossless crop / 90° rotation). Because base content fires all 33 variant names TINT-FREE, baking one colour per name is safe — the identity-white default tint multiplies through unchanged. COVERAGE IS PARTIAL AND DELIBERATE: a fired name is covered here only when a BulletPack design fits its orientation and size — directional families (needle/kunai/scale + player shots, orientToHeading) take elongated line/shot art rotated to +x (rule 7); radial families take compact round/oval art whose native size stays near a bullet's (≤~45px). The 11 fired names left uncovered keep aliasing to their reskinned floor cell (a proper BulletPack shape at a coherent size), and BulletPack's oversized beams (massive/streak/long lines) and its surplus per-level player shots have no bullet-sized home this round — they stage in extra/ for the laser and weapon rounds. EFFECTS — only the two explosions that reskin a death-site floor name (burst, burst.big) ship; the other six explosion strips reach no emit site (firing a pack-only fx name would crash the never-blocked floor), so they stage until a death/bomb tier fires them.
$variants: Enemy/boss fired names (src/render/procedural.ts BULLET_VARIANTS). Directional families set orientToHeading in base content, so their art is elongated and points +x; radial families keep native orientation. Each name here is one a real four-stage playthrough fires, so the baked design reaches play.

needle.vigil.note: sentinel 'Vigil Unbroken' beam — directional green line (+x)

needle.subpoena.note: stage-3 summons homing — directional yellow line (+x)

orb.small.writ.note: stage-3/4 aimed shot (most-fired) — one 13px round design cropped from a 306×17 choice-sheet

variants.$gem-reskins: 战役扩容轮: the 13 staged enemy-bullet files, sliced by EXPLICIT per-cell crop (never filename parsing — the sheets are uneven multi-hue choice-rows, verified by decoding each) into enumerated sibling variants keyed to a boss's gem identity (sentinel green, warden yellow, magistrate cyan, chancellor pink, regent purple; turret/bastion elite warm). Each variant re-skins ONE fired sub-pattern of that boss/elite (make-base-pack.ts), size-honestly: the crop's painted extent ≈ 2·radius of the bullet it lands on, so a Massive-family skin never oversells a small hitbox (design §a). Two files carry two slices each (Pink_Medium_Big → ledger+witness, Massive_purple_yellow → mandamus+halo.mandamus); every one of the 13 is consumed.

needle.tithe.note: sentinel SHARD re-skin — bright green dash (+x via orientToHeading), sliced from a 6-hue green choice-row

orb.small.beacon.note: turret shot re-skin (elite) — yellow orb ~9px on r3, sliced (the 44px orange middle is too big for any bullet — left unused)

needle.lien.note: warden SEEKER re-skin — thin yellow line (+x), one oval of a 5-hue row

orb.small.fee.note: warden SPARK re-skin — yellow orb ~8px on r3, the yellow slice of a mixed row

petal.pyre.note: censer EMBER re-skin — lava chunk ~14px on r3 (compact crop of the 54px blob); re-check test:density

orb.small.assay.note: bastion SHELL re-skin (elite) — warm-red orb ~11px on r4 (the 52px orange centre is too big — unused)

scale.escrow.note: magistrate SEEKER re-skin — cyan dart (+x), a 16px slice of a 36px cyan bar

orb.small.brief.note: chancellor WRIT re-skin — pink orb ~12px on r5, cropped from a 40px pink bar

orb.medium.ledger.note: chancellor DECREE re-skin — pink orb 13px on r6 (≈1:1 honest), 1st of two slices

halo.witness.note: chancellor SEAL re-skin — pink-purple orb 13px on r8, 2nd slice of the 7-orb pink→purple row

spark.docket.note: chancellor LEVY re-skin — pink spark ~10px on r5 (additive), cropped from a 56px pink bar

spark.duty.note: assessor LEVY re-skin — warm spark ~7px on r5 (additive), one of a 6-mote warm row

orb.medium.tenure.note: regent LATTICE re-skin — purple orb ~13px on r6, the purple half of a purple+tan row

orb.medium.mandamus.note: regent DECREE re-skin — purple orb 14px on r6, 1st of two slices from a 6-orb purple row

halo.mandamus.note: regent CROWN re-skin — bright purple orb 14px on r7 (≈1:1 honest), 2nd slice

$shots: Player weapon and option fired names (BULLET_VARIANTS player section). Directional shots are drawn nose-up in BulletPack and rotated 90° to +x (rule 7) so orientToHeading points them along the firing heading; the radial spread bolt and the option shots keep native orientation. NOTE (laser round, 2026-07-22): GUN_BEAM no longer fires the bullet-cell name 'glow.small.beam' — the player's beam now wears the laser SKIN 'beam.cyan' (assets.lasers, backed by laser_cian_strip10), so its old backing file 'P1 Bullet Stream.png' is unmapped here and STAGES for the later player-weapon round. The 'glow.small.beam' variant alias survives in procedural.ts but is fired by nothing.

glow.small.bolt.note: GUN_BOLT spread — radial animated amber spiky ball

shots.$tier-skins: 战役扩容轮: the 12 staged player-bullet files onto per-tier shot skins. The `Player.#shot()` seam indexes levels[floor(power)], so a shot naming a different sprite per tier grows visibly as power climbs — presentation only, every number byte-identical (make-base-pack.ts swaps only style.sprite per level). NO displacement: every EXISTING consumed backer keeps its slot (spread t0 glow.small.bolt, needle t0 needle.pin, homing t0/t1 scale.tracker, the four options) — these new names fill only the tier slots that were duplicate/aliased-floor. Cian lvl0/1/2 climb scout/spread t1-t3 (+ Hyper is spread's maxed); Pink lvl0/1/2 climb lance/needle t1-t3; hound/homing t2-t3 take chase (BermudaGreen green + Alt2 big); maw/scatter t0-t3 take the spray ladder (Stream, Yellow, p1_Alt, Cian lvl2). Spread/scatter shots are radial (fire up, no orientToHeading) so their vertical art needs NO rotate; needle/homing shots set orientToHeading so their art is rotated 90° to +x (rule 7), exactly as needle.pin/scale.tracker already are.

glow.medium.bolt.note: spread t1 — cyan bolt lvl0 (2-frame flicker, up = no rotate)

bolt.hyper.note: spread t3 maxed — the big hyper bolt (draw size capped honest via style.width in make-base-pack)

glow.small.spray.note: scatter t0 — ember pellet (radial 16px, no rotate); was an aliased floor, now baked

glow.small.spray.t3.note: scatter t3 — the biggest cyan bolt as the maxed spray core

$lasers: LASER body/cap strips → assets.lasers (laser round, 2026-07-22). Keyed by laser-strip name (src/render/laser-skin.ts): 8 body strips (each named by exactly one skin) + 3 cap strips (each shared by ≥1 skin), all 11 BulletPack Lasers/ files. Emitted BAKED (color 'baked', no whiten — the coloured art the artist drew, saturation gate skipped) as one PNG per strip, each frame rotated 90° to +x on import (rule 7 — the source beams run long-axis-vertical; the renderer then uses the one convention, length along +x, no runtime transpose). The base campaign FIRES all 8 body skins (LANCE→beam.v3, warden/magistrate COLUMN→beam.heavy/blue/cyan, ray→beam.slim, chancellor→beam.warm/stream/v3.stream, player GUN_BEAM→beam.cyan), so a baked strip reaches real play the moment it is keyed by the skin's body/cap name — no companion content pack needed.

beam.v3.note: LANCE / lash beam body — whole-beam v3 (stretch)

beam.slim.note: ray swept beam body — slim green tileable (the beam-sweep hero)

beam.heavy.note: warden 'Picket Line' beam body — heavy green tileable

beam.cyan.note: magistrate 'Assize' + player GUN_BEAM body — whole-beam cyan (stretch)

beam.warm.note: chancellor 'Sweeping Assay' rake body — warm red tileable

beam.stream.note: chancellor curtain stream body — green tileable

beam.v3.stream.note: chancellor stream wall body — v3 tileable

variantsDuplicate.$comment: Files that are byte-for-byte duplicates of a consumed source; represented by the named variant, not packed twice.

$effects: Only names a real death/detonation site fires ship as pack effects. `burst` (enemy death) and `burst.big` (boss/player death) RESKIN procedural fx-floor names the base campaign already emits (src/game/run.ts; effectAtlas composites a reskinned floor name from the pack's native pixels — src/render/procedural.ts nativeEffectAtlas). The three `missile.pop.tiny|mid|big` tiers RESKIN the missile-detonation fx-floor names a base missile fires on death (src/render/procedural.ts FX_STRIPS, src/sim/bullet.ts MissileSpec.explosion) — sourced from the Missiles/ folder's Exp strips, not Explosions/. Frame counts land frame-for-frame on the floor (tiny 11, mid 9, big 8; the floor tracks the file). The base effect's `life` is fixed (burst 24, burst.big 36 ticks; the pop tiers' life is their strip length, src/sim/effects.ts), so ticksPerFrame lands the animation near that window (pop tiny/mid 2, big 3, mirroring src/render/procedural.ts popStrip). The other six Explosions/ strips are NOT mapped: firing a pack-only fx name from a base death site would crash the never-blocked floor (main.ts throws on an unresolved sprite), so they have no consumer until a death/bomb tier with its own procedural floor is added. They stage in extra/explosions/ and are counted staged, not consumed.

burst.note: RESKIN of the enemy-death flash (base effect 'burst', life 24)

burst.big.note: RESKIN of the boss/player-death flash (base effect 'burst.big', life 36): expanding shockwave

missile.pop.tiny.note: RESKIN of the small missile detonation (fx floor 'missile.pop.tiny', 11 frames)

missile.pop.mid.note: RESKIN of the mid missile detonation (fx floor 'missile.pop.mid', 9 frames)

missile.pop.big.note: RESKIN of the big missile detonation (fx floor 'missile.pop.big', 8 frames)

effects.$death-tiers: 战役扩容轮: the 6 staged Explosions/ strips RESKIN the death-explosion fx-floor names the systems stage authored (src/render/procedural.ts FX_STRIPS; src/game/deathfx.ts routes a death site to its tier). Frame counts and ticksPerFrame land frame-for-frame on the floor so the animation completes within the effect's fixed `life` (src/sim/effects.ts): boom.elite 28×1=28, boom.elite.spray 39×1=39, boom.boss.back 15×3=45, boom.boss.top 16×3=48, boom.player 38×2=76. boom.boss.back is the normal-blend occluding plate (Layer.BurstsBack); the rest are additive. debris is the loop ember from the 2×2 Versatile sheet — its 2px frame is sub-FX_PAD, so it is NEAREST-upscaled 4× to 8×8 at bake (design §c), the seam gate untouched.

boom.elite.note: RESKIN of the elite death core (fx floor 'boom.elite', 28 frames, life 28)

boom.elite.spray.note: RESKIN of the elite death spray (fx floor 'boom.elite.spray', 39 frames, life 39)

boom.boss.back.note: RESKIN of the boss-blast back plate (fx floor 'boom.boss.back', 15 frames, life 45) — normal blend, Layer.BurstsBack

boom.boss.top.note: RESKIN of the boss-blast top flash (fx floor 'boom.boss.top', 16 frames, life 48)

boom.player.note: RESKIN of the player death (fx floor 'boom.player', 38 frames, life 76)

debris.note: RESKIN of the debris ember (fx floor 'debris', 12-frame loop) — 2×2 source NEAREST-upscaled 4× to 8×8 (sub-FX_PAD, design §c)

$missiles: MISSILE body strips → assets.missiles (import round, 2026-07-22). Keyed by missile-body name (src/render/procedural.ts MISSILE_STRIPS): 12 `strip5` bodies (missile.0 … missile.11) + 1 `strip17` (missile.massive), all 13 BulletPack Missiles/ body files (the 3 Exp detonation strips are effects, above). Emitted BAKED (color 'baked', no whiten — the coloured art the artist drew, saturation gate skipped) as one PNG per strip, each frame rotated 90° to +x on import (rule 7 — the source bodies are drawn nose-UP; the renderer then uses the one convention, length along +x, no runtime transpose). The mapping is DELIBERATELY index-matched: MissileN_strip5 → missile.N, Massive_Missile_strip17 → missile.massive — the procedural floor's per-body frame sizes (bodyGeo/massiveGeo) were derived from exactly these files' native sizes after the +90° rotation, so a baked body lands on its floor's proportions. The base campaign FIRES all 13 bodies (each stage's already-reached missile firers name one, src/packs/base-pack.json), so a baked body reaches real play the moment it is keyed by the body name a spec fires — no companion content pack needed. ticksPerFrame mirrors the floor (bodies 3, massive 2; src/render/procedural.ts bodyGeo/massiveGeo), mode 'loop' (the thruster flicker).

missile.massive.note: final-boss signature writ — nose-up 44×71 → +x, twin-thruster 17-frame loop

$pickups: PICKUP coin/gem/bar body strips → assets.pickups (pickup-variety round, 2026-07-22). Keyed by pickup-skin name (src/render/procedural.ts PICKUP_STRIPS): 2 coins (6-frame disc) + 5 gems (9-frame facet) + 1 bar (9-frame ingot) = 8 of the 10 BulletPack Misc/ files. Emitted BAKED (color 'baked', no whiten — the coloured coins/gems the artist drew, saturation gate skipped) as one PNG per strip, RADIAL (no rotate — a coin has no heading, rule 7 is moot), mode 'loop' (the spin), through the same per-file build/seam gate effects use. A base item names one of these skins TINT-FREE (the denomination tint colours only the glow halo), so a baked coin reaches real play the moment its strip is keyed by the name an item's sprite fires — no companion content pack. TWO specifics: (1) 'Cian' is the file's own spelling of cyan → pickup.gem.cyan (typo preserved at the source, corrected in the engine name). (2) SUFFIX TRAP: Silver_coin_NoShadow_strip.png carries NO _stripN suffix (the auto-parser would read frames:1, frameW:108), so its 'strip': 6 is HAND-DECLARED here by analogy to its shadowed twin (6 frames of 18px) — self-describing metadata, never inferred from the filename. The 2 SHADOWED coin twins (Gold_coin_strip6, Silver_coin_strip6) are DELIBERATELY UNMAPPED and stage in extra/misc/: the uniform no-baked-shadow policy (a shadow authored for a light background reads as a grey smudge on the dark curtain; the itemGlow halo already supplies lift) — pack-author alternate skins, deferred with reason, not silently dropped.

pickup.coin.silver.note: score chip = silver coin — HAND-DECLARED 6 frames (no _stripN suffix; the auto-parser would read 1×108)

pickup.gem.green.note: gem.green (sentinel signature) — 9-frame facet spin (-sheet suffix is export noise)

pickup.gem.cyan.note: gem.cyan (magistrate signature) — 9-frame facet spin ('Cian' is the file's spelling of cyan)

pickup.gem.pink.note: gem.pink (chancellor signature) — 9-frame facet spin (no -sheet suffix, same 9×15×15)

pickups.$tally-coins: 战役扩容轮: the 2 shadowed coin twins leave the staged pile at last. Their baked shadow is wrong on the dark FIELD (the no-baked-shadow policy that deferred them), but CORRECT on the ALL CLEAR / GAME OVER results card — a lit UI surface with an implied light where a coin's cast shadow reads honestly. They floor as pickup.tally.coin.gold/silver (src/render/procedural.ts, systems stage) and are drawn on the ending card's coin tally (src/game/states.ts coinTally, src/main.ts drawCoinTally). 6-frame spin, radial (no rotate).

pickup.tally.coin.gold.note: results-card gold tally — the shadowed twin, lit-surface-correct

pickup.tally.coin.silver.note: results-card silver tally — the shadowed twin, lit-surface-correct
